goal: Learn to build GenAI applications using the Claude API and Claude Code

original_scope.1: Get plan generation working, focus on prompt design and structured output
original_scope.2: Add persistence and logging, learn state management across sessions
original_scope.3: Add replanning, start eval work — is the new plan better than the original?
original_scope.4: Clean up, document, write the GitHub post

1: PROJECT: Build a multi-turn CLI research assistant that maintains conversation context, streams responses, and persists session history to SQLite
Implement streaming response handler using anthropic.messages.stream() with live token output to stdout
Design a Message object schema and SQLite persistence layer that stores full conversation history with timestamps and token usage
Build conversation context manager that retrieves and injects prior turns respecting the 200k context window limits
Add a --resume flag that reloads any prior session by ID and continues the conversation from where it left off
Instrument token usage tracking per turn and print a running cost estimate using Claude 3.5 Sonnet pricing
2: PROJECT: Build an agentic document intelligence pipeline that uses tool use to extract structured data from PDFs, cross-reference against a database, and produce a validated JSON report
Define a custom tool schema for document parsing operations: extract_table, extract_entities, query_database, and validate_schema
Implement the tool execution loop with proper ToolUseBlock and ToolResultBlock handling including error propagation back to the model
Write a PDF-to-vision pipeline using base64-encoded page images passed to Claude's vision input for scanned document support
Build the cross-reference tool that queries a local DuckDB database and returns structured results the model can reason over
Add an agentic retry layer: if Claude's output fails JSON schema validation, automatically re-prompt with the validation errors until it passes or hits 3 attempts
3: PROJECT: Deploy a production-grade FastAPI service wrapping Claude that supports parallel async requests, prompt caching, and a configurable system prompt library with A/B testing instrumentation
Build async FastAPI endpoints using anthropic.AsyncAnthropic with asyncio.gather for concurrent multi-request handling and benchmark throughput
Implement prompt caching using cache_control breakpoints on system prompts and large static context blocks, measure cache hit rates via API response headers
Create a YAML-defined system prompt library with named variants and wire up a request-level A/B assignment that logs variant, latency, and output tokens to Postgres
Add a middleware layer for request authentication, rate limiting per API key, and structured JSON logging compatible with a log aggregator
Write a /eval endpoint that accepts a test dataset, runs all prompt variants in parallel, scores outputs with a Claude-as-judge call, and returns a comparison report
4: PROJECT: Build and deploy a Claude Code-powered autonomous code review agent that clones a GitHub repo, performs multi-file analysis using extended thinking, and posts actionable PR comments via the GitHub API
Use Claude Code SDK to orchestrate a headless agent session that accepts a GitHub PR URL, clones the diff, and maps changed files into a structured analysis plan
Enable extended thinking (budget_tokens: 8000) for architectural reasoning tasks where the agent must trace data flow across multiple files before forming a judgment
Implement a multi-agent subagent pattern: a coordinator Claude instance decomposes the PR into review domains (security, performance, correctness) and spawns parallel specialist agents for each
Build a GitHub API integration that deduplicates comments, maps findings to specific line numbers using the pull_request_review API, and posts a summary review with severity-weighted scoring

Add a self-critique loop where the agent reviews its own draft comments with a second Claude call, filters out low-confidence findings below a threshold, and logs precision metrics for future calibration

1: Introduction to Generative AI concepts and Large Language Models (LLMs) — read overview articles and watch intro videos (2h)
Create an Anthropic account, explore the Claude API documentation, and understand API key setup and authentication (2h)
Study Claude's core API endpoints: messages, completions, and tokens — run your first 'Hello World' API call using curl or Postman (2h)
Learn Python basics for API integration: requests library, environment variables with dotenv, and error handling (2h)
Build a simple CLI chatbot using the Claude API — single-turn and multi-turn conversation with message history (2h)
2: Deep dive into prompt engineering: system prompts, user/assistant roles, and best practices for Claude (2h)
Explore advanced prompting techniques: chain-of-thought, few-shot examples, XML tags for structured input, and temperature/top-p parameters (2h)
Learn Claude's tool use (function calling) feature — define tools, handle tool calls, and return tool results in a conversation loop (2h)
Build a practical tool-use application: a weather assistant or calculator bot that uses external functions via Claude's tool use API (2h)
Introduction to Claude Code — install the CLI, understand its architecture, and run your first Claude Code session in a project directory (2h)
3: Explore Claude Code workflows: slash commands, file context, code generation, explanation, and debugging features (2h)
Use Claude Code to scaffold a small web application — practice iterative development, ask Claude to write tests, and refactor code (2h)
Build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline: chunk documents, generate embeddings, store in a vector DB (e.g., ChromaDB), and query with Claude (2h)
Add streaming responses to your Claude API app using the streaming API — improve UX with real-time token output in a terminal or web UI (2h)
Build a document Q&A application combining RAG + streaming + tool use — end-to-end mini project integrating Week 1–3 skills (2h)
4: Learn best practices for production Claude apps: rate limiting, retry logic, cost estimation, token counting, and logging (2h)
Explore Anthropic's safety guidelines, responsible AI use, and how to implement content moderation in your applications (2h)
Build a full-stack GenAI web app: use FastAPI or Flask backend with Claude API + a simple React or HTML/JS frontend (2h)
Use Claude Code to enhance and debug your full-stack app — practice agentic coding workflows, automated refactoring, and writing documentation (2h)
Review all projects, deploy your web app (e.g., to Render or Railway), write a README, and outline next steps for advanced topics like agents and multi-modal Claude (2h)
